office_does: One of two House seats for LD 43 (central Seattle); votes on state law, the state budget, and taxes.
race_blurb: Single-candidate race: first-term incumbent Rep. Shaun Scott is the only candidate on the primary ballot and advances to the general automatically.

candidates.summary: First-term incumbent elected in 2024, Vice Chair of the House Labor & Workplace Standards Committee, and the first open socialist in the Washington Legislature since the 1910s.
highlights: Credits corporate tax-break repeal work with preserving $645 million in biennial spending on housing, wildfire mitigation, and schools [S1]
Passed a bill requiring large financial institutions to pay standard document recording fees, funding affordable housing [S3]
Seattle DSA member; Vice Chair of the House Labor & Workplace Standards Committee [S1][S3]

taxes.basis: Record of ending corporate tax breaks to preserve $645 million in spending on public services.

spending.basis: Record and framing center on preserving and expanding public investment in housing, schools, and higher education.

housing.basis: Passed document recording fee parity bill generating tens of millions annually for affordable housing.

climate.basis: Sponsored wildfire response funding; preserved wildfire-mitigation spending; endorsed by Washington Conservation Action.

experience.basis: First-term incumbent with passed bills and a committee vice-chairmanship, but a short legislative tenure.

reform.basis: Frames major corporate lobbying as an oligarchic threat to public services.

social.basis: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates across his two campaigns.
